Class SerialPrimitive

java.lang.Object
me.apotterhead.cheers.vars.SerialPrimitive
All Implemented Interfaces:
SerialVariable
public class SerialPrimitive extends Object implements SerialVariable
SerialPrimitive is a class that represents a variable that is a primitive datatype.

SerialObjectVariable implements SerialVariable and helps act as an intermediary between the variables in the orignal Object and the serialzied String.

For the purpose of this library, String is considered a primitive datatype desipite its internal implementation. Similarly, any null value that exists is treated as a primitive type.

Since:
1.0.0
See Also:
  • SerialObjectVariable

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType
    SerialPrimitiveType is an enum that represents the datatype of a primitive variable.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SerialPrimitive(String name)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.NULL with the provided name and value of "null".
    SerialPrimitive(String name, boolean value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.BOOLEAN with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, byte value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.BYTE with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, char value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.CHAR with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, double value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.DOUBLE with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, float value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.FLOAT with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, int value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.INT with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, long value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.LONG with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, short value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.SHORT with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, String value)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.STRING with the provided name and String representation of value.
    SerialPrimitive(String name, String value, SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType type)
    Constructs a new SerialPrimitive with the provided name, value and type.

  • Constructor Details

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, boolean value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.BOOLEAN with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the boolean value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, byte value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.BYTE with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the byte value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, short value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.SHORT with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the short value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, int value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.INT with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the int value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, long value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.LONG with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the long value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, float value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.FLOAT with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the float value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, double value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.DOUBLE with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the double value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, char value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.CHAR with the provided name and String representation of value.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the char value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, String value)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.STRING with the provided name and String representation of value.

      The String provided for value will be encoded with the following substitutions for common escape characters: \\, \n, \t, \", \r, \b, \f

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the String value of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive where type=SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType.NULL with the provided name and value of "null".
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized

    • SerialPrimitive

      public SerialPrimitive(String name, String value, SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType type)
      Constructs a new SerialPrimitive with the provided name, value and type. There is zero type validation when this constructor is used, and it is meant only for internal use.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the variable in the Object being serialized
      value - the value of the variable in the Object being serialized. Although it is a String, it does not go through the same process of encoding as SerialPrimitive(String, String) and is ment for raw data.
      type - the type of the variable in the Object being serialized

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns a String representation of the variable's name. This must match exactly what the name of the variable was in the original Object in order to be propperly deserialized.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface SerialVariable
      Returns:
      a String representation of the variable's name

    • getType

      public SerialPrimitive.SerialPrimitiveType getType()
      Returns a SerialPrimitiveType representing the datatype of the variable.
      Returns:
      a SerialPrimitiveType representing the datatype of the variable

    • getBoolean

      public boolean getBoolean()
      Returns the value of the variable as a boolean. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a boolean

    • getByte

      public byte getByte()
      Returns the value of the variable as a byte. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a byte

    • getShort

      public short getShort()
      Returns the value of the variable as a short. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a short

    • getInt

      public int getInt()
      Returns the value of the variable as an int. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as an int

    • getLong

      public long getLong()
      Returns the value of the variable as a long. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a long

    • getFloat

      public float getFloat()
      Returns the value of the variable as a float. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a float

    • getDouble

      public double getDouble()
      Returns the value of the variable as a double. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a double

    • getChar

      public char getChar()
      Returns the value of the variable as a char. This method does not contain any type validation and will throw an error if there is a datatype mismatch.
      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a char

    • getString

      public String getString()
      Returns the value of the variable as a String.

      The String returned by this method is decoded with the following substitutions for common escape characters: \\, \n, \t, \", \r, \b, \f

      Returns:
      the value of the variable as a Stirng

    • getRawValue

      public String getRawValue()
      Returns value as a String. This method does not perform any modifications on value as its stored internally, and may not trully the variable it is supposed to.
      Returns:
      the raw value of the variable as a String

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a serialized version of the variable. Every variable in the final product returnd by Serializer.serialize(Object, Version) is generated by this method.

      Each implementation should return in the format of "name:TYPE:value". For the case of SerialPrimitive, "TYPE" is determined by calling Enum.name()

      Specified by:
      toString in interface SerialVariable
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a String representation of the variable. This is what is written to represent the variable in the serialization process.}
